hypothesis: If a hybrid Transformer-Mamba encoder-decoder foundation model is pre-trained jointly on multiple molecular representation modalities (SMILES, SELFIES, InChI, IUPAC name, molecular formula, polymer, and electrolyte representations) using aligned multimodal objectives, then it will learn a unified latent space that captures cross-modal molecular semantics, enabling superior performance on molecular property prediction, representation translation, and clustering tasks compared to single-modality approaches, because joint multimodal training forces the model to align complementary structural and chemical information encoded across different molecular notation systems.
